title: Thermoelectric elements and a temperature condition for clear ice making
abstract: An ice making apparatus for an appliance includes a housing that has an interior volume and an ice tray horizontally suspended across the interior volume that is configured to retain water. The ice making apparatus also includes a heat pump thermally coupled to a bottom surface of the ice tray. The heat pump is configured to freeze water in the ice tray and expel heat. A heat transfer device is configured to move heat expelled by the heat pump to an upper portion of the interior volume.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An ice making apparatus for an appliance, comprising: a housing surrounding an interior volume; an ice tray horizontally suspended across the interior volume and configured to retain water; a thermoelectric device having a cold side and a hot side, the cold side thermally coupled to a bottom portion of the ice tray; and an air movement device configured to circulate air within the interior volume, such that the air transfers heat from the hot side of the thermoelectric device to an upper portion of the ice tray. 2. The ice making apparatus of claim 1, wherein the thermoelectric device hot side is thermally coupled to a heat sink. 3. The ice making apparatus of any one of claims 1-2 further comprising: a thermal transfer device thermally coupled to the heat sink, wherein the thermal transfer device is at least partially disposed over the ice tray. 4. The ice making apparatus of claim 3, wherein the thermal transfer device is at least one of a heat pipe and a thermal siphon. 5. The ice making apparatus of claim 3 further comprising: a thermal storage element disposed within the interior volume of the housing for stabilizing the air temperature within the housing; and an insulative material positioned between the housing and the thermal storage element. 6. The ice making apparatus of any one of claims 1-2 further comprising: a thermal storage element disposed within the interior volume of the housing for stabilizing the air temperature within the housing. 7. The ice making apparatus of claim 6, wherein the thermal storage element is disposed above the ice tray. 8. The ice making apparatus of claim 6, wherein the thermal storage element comprises a material that undergoes a phase transition at a temperature above 0Â° Celsius. 9. The ice making apparatus of claim 6, wherein the thermal storage element is configured to absorb and expel heat such that the air temperature within the housing is stabilized above 0Â° Celsius. 10. The ice making apparatus of claim 7, wherein the thermal storage element comprises a material that undergoes a phase transition at a temperature above 0" Celsius. 11. The ice making apparatus of claim 10, wherein the material of the thermal storage element comprises at least one of a wax, a metal, and a refrigerant and the ice making apparatus further comprises an insulative material positioned between the housing and the thermal storage element. 12. The ice making apparatus of claim 5, wherein the thermal transfer device includes at least one of an air movement device, a thermal siphon, and a heat pipe. 13. The ice making apparatus of claim 12, wherein the thermal transfer device is a heat pipe chosen from the group consisting of a vapor chamber, a variable conductance heat pipe, a pressure controlled heat pipe, and a diode heat pipe. 14. The ice making apparatus of claim 13, wherein the housing includes a body portion, an opening, and a door connected with a hinge permitting the housing to open via the door rotating away from the body portion; and wherein the housing encloses the ice tray when the door is closed and allows for clear ice to be delivered to an ice storage space of an ice cube storage container; and wherein the ice making apparatus is positioned within a door of a refrigeration appliance. 15. A method comprising the steps of: providing an appliance with an ice maker housing that has an interior volume; providing an ice tray suspended within the interior volume, the ice tray having reservoirs containing water and a bottom surface thermally coupled to a thermoelectric device; transferring heat from the bottom surface of the ice tray across the thermoelectric device to air below the ice tray within the interior volume; circulating the air such that heat from the bottom surface is transferred to a top surface of the water; and forming substantially clear ice pieces in the ice tray. 16. An ice making apparatus for an appliance, comprising: a housing having an interior volume; an ice tray contained within the interior volume and configured to retain water; a heat pump thermally coupled to a bottom surface of the ice tray, the heat pump configured to expel heat away from the bottom surface and freeze water in the ice tray; and 

 a heat transfer device configured to move heat expelled by the heat pump to an upper portion of the interior volume for forming at least one substantially clear ice piece within the ice tray. 17. The ice making apparatus of claim 16 further comprising: a thermal storage element disposed within the internal volume of the housing. 18. The ice making apparatus of claim 17, wherein the thermal storage element comprises a material that undergoes a phase transition at a temperature above 0Â° Celsius. 19. The ice making apparatus of claim 17, wherein the material of the thermal storage element comprises at least one of a wax, a metal, and a refrigerant. 20. The ice making apparatus of claim 16, wherein the heat transfer device includes at least one of an air movement device, a thermal siphon, and a heat pipe. 21. The ice making apparatus of claim 16, wherein the heat pump is thermally coupled to a heat sink. 22. The ice making apparatus of claim 21, wherein the heat transfer device is thermally coupled to the heat sink. 23. The ice making apparatus of claim 22, wherein the heat transfer device is at least partially disposed above the ice tray. 24. The ice making apparatus of claim 21, wherein the heat transfer device circulates air within the internal volume. 25. A method comprising the steps of: providing a refrigerator with a door that includes an ice maker housing positioned within the door where the ice maker housing has an interior volume; providing an ice tray suspended within the interior volume, the ice tray having reservoirs containing water and a bottom surface thermally coupled to a thermoelectric device; transferring heat from the bottom surface of the ice tray across the thermoelectric device to air below the ice tray within the interior volume; circulating the air such that heat from the bottom surface is transferred to a top surface of the water; and forming substantially clear ice pieces in the ice tray. 26. The method of claim 25 further comprising the steps of: transferring heat from the circulating air across the thermoelectric device to the bottom surface of the ice tray for releasing the substantially clear ice pieces from the tray; and providing a thermal storage element within the interior volume for stabilizing air temperature in an upper portion of the interior volume. 27. The method of claim 25 further comprising the step of: providing a thermal transfer device thermally coupled to the thermoelectric device, wherein the thermal transfer device transfers heat from the thermoelectric device to an upper portion of the interior housing. 28. The method of any of claims 25-27, wherein the ice tray has a bottom and a top and the step of forming substantially clear ice pieces in the ice tray forms clear ice pieces by directionally solidifying the water into ice from the bottom of the ice tray to the top of the ice tray. 29. The method of any of claims 26-27, wherein the thermal storage element absorbs and expels heat such that the air temperature within the housing is stabilized above 0Â° Celsius. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the ice tray has a bottom and a top and the step of forming substantially clear ice pieces in the ice tray directionally solidifies the water into ice from the bottom of the ice tray to the top of the ice tray. 31. The method of any of claims 26-27, wherein thermal storage element is disposed within the interior volume of the housing for stabilizing the air temperature within the housing; and an insulative material positioned between the housing and the thermal storage element.
